SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.coordinator

Interface ICoordinator

All Known Implementing Classes:
Coordinator
public interface ICoordinator
Interface for the Smart Home Coordinator node.

The Coordinator is the central piece of the system. A cluster of Coordinator nodes runs leader election on startup; only the elected leader actively processes user requests and publishes commands. All nodes passively track system state via the message bus so any node can take over immediately if the leader fails.

Responsibilities:

  • Participate in and manage leader election.
  • Register edge devices as they come online.
  • Receive, validate, and dispatch user commands.
  • Forward automation rules to the Automation Hub.
  • Monitor device liveness via heartbeats.
  • Expose the current leader's identity to the API Gateway.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addAutomationRule(AutomationRule rule)
    Validates, stores, and publishes a new automation rule.
    List<Device>
    getAllDevices()
    Returns all devices currently registered with the system.
    String
    getCurrentLeaderId()
    Returns the node ID of the currently elected leader coordinator.
    Device
    getDeviceById(String deviceId)
    Returns a specific registered device by its ID.
    void
    handleAutomationMessage(AutomationRuleMessage message)
    Called by the AUTOMATION topic subscriber when an AutomationRuleMessage arrives from the leader.
    CommandStatus
    handleCommand(Command command)
    Processes a user-initiated command request.
    void
    handleCommandResult(String commandId, CommandStatus status)
    Handles the result of a previously issued command received from the message bus.
    void
    handleDeviceHeartbeat(String deviceId)
    Processes a heartbeat message received from an edge device.
    void
    handleNodeHeartbeat(String nodeId)
    Processes a heartbeat received from another coordinator node.
    boolean
    isLeader()
    Returns whether this coordinator instance is the current leader.
    void
    receiveCoordinator(String newLeaderId)
    Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when a COORDINATOR broadcast arrives.
    void
    receiveElection(String fromNodeId)
    Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when an ELECTION message arrives from a lower-priority peer node.
    void
    receiveOk(String fromNodeId)
    Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when an OK message arrives targeted at this node.
    void
    registerDevice(Device device)
    Handles a device registration event received from the REGISTER topic.
    void
    removeAutomationRule(String ruleId)
    Removes an existing automation rule and notifies all subscribers.
    void
    start()
    Starts this coordinator node: registers with the message bus, subscribes to relevant topics (REGISTER, HEARTBEAT, COMMAND results), and initiates the leader election process.
    void
    startElection()
    Starts a new leader election by launching a LeaderElectionRunner in a dedicated thread.
    void
    stop()
    Gracefully shuts down this coordinator node.
    void
    toggleAutomationRule(String ruleId, boolean active)
    Enables or disables an existing automation rule without removing it.

  • Method Details

    • start

      void start()
      Starts this coordinator node: registers with the message bus, subscribes to relevant topics (REGISTER, HEARTBEAT, COMMAND results), and initiates the leader election process.

    • stop

      void stop()
      Gracefully shuts down this coordinator node. Unregisters from the message bus and notifies peers.

    • startElection

      void startElection()
      Starts a new leader election by launching a LeaderElectionRunner in a dedicated thread.

      Called on startup and by HeartbeatMonitor when the leader's heartbeat timeout elapses. No-op if an election thread is already running.

    • receiveElection

      void receiveElection(String fromNodeId)
      Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when an ELECTION message arrives from a lower-priority peer node.

      Per the Bully Algorithm, this node must:

      1. Reply with an OK message targeted at fromNodeId.
      2. Call startElection() if not already running one, since this node has higher priority and should assert leadership.
      Parameters:
      fromNodeId - the node ID of the peer that sent the ELECTION message

    • receiveOk

      void receiveOk(String fromNodeId)
      Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when an OK message arrives targeted at this node.

      Signals the active LeaderElectionRunner that a higher-priority node is alive via electionRunner.receiveOk(), causing it to stand down.

      Parameters:
      fromNodeId - the node ID of the higher-priority peer that sent OK

    • receiveCoordinator

      void receiveCoordinator(String newLeaderId)
      Called by the ELECTION topic subscriber when a COORDINATOR broadcast arrives.

      Updates this node's local currentLeaderId to newLeaderId and cancels any in-progress election thread. If this node is the new leader, activates leader-only responsibilities: starts the AutomationHub thread and begins accepting requests from the API Gateway.

      Parameters:
      newLeaderId - the node ID of the newly elected leader

    • getCurrentLeaderId

      String getCurrentLeaderId()
      Returns the node ID of the currently elected leader coordinator. All nodes — leader and follower — maintain this value. The API Gateway calls this on any coordinator node to discover where to route requests.
      Returns:
      the node ID of the current leader, or null if no election has completed yet

    • isLeader

      boolean isLeader()
      Returns whether this coordinator instance is the current leader.
      Returns:
      true if this node's ID equals currentLeaderId

    • registerDevice

      void registerDevice(Device device)
      Handles a device registration event received from the REGISTER topic. Creates a Device record and stores it in the local registry. All coordinator nodes call this so every node maintains full state.
      Parameters:
      device - the device that has registered itself

    • getAllDevices

      List<Device> getAllDevices()
      Returns all devices currently registered with the system.
      Returns:
      a list of all known Device instances

    • getDeviceById

      Device getDeviceById(String deviceId)
      Returns a specific registered device by its ID.
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device to look up
      Returns:
      the Device with the given ID, or null if not found

    • handleCommand

      CommandStatus handleCommand(Command command)
      Processes a user-initiated command request. Validates the device and action, publishes the command to the COMMAND topic on the message bus, and returns the resulting status once the device responds.

      Only the current leader should call this method.

      Parameters:
      command - the command to dispatch
      Returns:
      the CommandStatus after the command has been processed
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if called on a non-leader node

    • handleCommandResult

      void handleCommandResult(String commandId, CommandStatus status)
      Handles the result of a previously issued command received from the message bus. Updates the command's status and forwards the result to the API Gateway.
      Parameters:
      commandId - the ID of the command whose result was received
      status - the outcome of the command execution

    • addAutomationRule

      void addAutomationRule(AutomationRule rule)
      Validates, stores, and publishes a new automation rule.

      Only the current leader should call this method. The leader:

      1. Validates that the trigger device type and target device exist in the device registry.
      2. Stores the rule in its own local automation rule list.
      3. Publishes an AutomationRuleMessage with operation ADD to the TopicType.AUTOMATION topic so follower coordinators mirror it and the Automation Hub activates it.
      4. Returns success to the API Gateway once the publish is confirmed.
      Parameters:
      rule - the automation rule to add
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if called on a non-leader node

    • removeAutomationRule

      void removeAutomationRule(String ruleId)
      Removes an existing automation rule and notifies all subscribers.

      Only the current leader should call this method. The leader:

      1. Removes the rule from its own local automation rule list.
      2. Publishes an AutomationRuleMessage with operation REMOVE to the TopicType.AUTOMATION topic so follower coordinators remove it and the Automation Hub deactivates it.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to remove
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if called on a non-leader node

    • toggleAutomationRule

      void toggleAutomationRule(String ruleId, boolean active)
      Enables or disables an existing automation rule without removing it.

      Only the current leader should call this method. The leader:

      1. Updates the rule's active flag in its own local automation rule list.
      2. Publishes an AutomationRuleMessage with operation TOGGLE to the TopicType.AUTOMATION topic so follower coordinators and the Automation Hub apply the same state change.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to toggle
      active - true to enable the rule, false to disable it
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if called on a non-leader node

    • handleAutomationMessage

      void handleAutomationMessage(AutomationRuleMessage message)
      Called by the AUTOMATION topic subscriber when an AutomationRuleMessage arrives from the leader.

      Called on all nodes — leader and follower — since all coordinator nodes subscribe to the AUTOMATION topic for state replication. The method inspects the operation type and applies the corresponding change to the local automation rule list:

      • ADD — adds the rule to the local list.
      • REMOVE — removes the rule from the local list by ID.
      • TOGGLE — updates the rule's active flag in the local list.

      Note: on the leader node this is effectively a no-op since the leader already applied the change before publishing. On follower nodes this is the primary mechanism for keeping automation state in sync.

      Parameters:
      message - the automation rule operation message received from the bus

    • handleDeviceHeartbeat

      void handleDeviceHeartbeat(String deviceId)
      Processes a heartbeat message received from an edge device. Updates the device's last-seen timestamp and marks it as online.
      Parameters:
      deviceId - the ID of the device that sent the heartbeat

    • handleNodeHeartbeat

      void handleNodeHeartbeat(String nodeId)
      Processes a heartbeat received from another coordinator node. Used to detect leader failure. If the leader's heartbeat is missed, triggers a new leader election.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the ID of the coordinator node that sent the heartbeat
